summary: Drop the pre-marshal of bodyArgs in makeAPIHandler so MCP POST/PUT/PATCH send JSON objects, not base64 strings.
reason: client.Post/Put/Patch take `body any` and json-marshal it inside do(). makeAPIHandler was calling `json.Marshal(bodyArgs)` first and passing the resulting []byte; Go re-encodes []byte as a base64 JSON string, so every mutating MCP tool sent a quoted base64 blob (e.g. "eyJ0b2tlbiI6Li4ufQ==") instead of the intended JSON object — Pushover rejected all ~20 mutating MCP tools (messages.send, groups.add_user, licenses.assign, etc). Same pre-marshal pattern reproduces in 38 published CLIs; this is a template-level bug filed upstream (cli-printing-press#TBD) but patched locally first so the published pushover MCP works.
validated_outcome: go test ./internal/mcp -run TestMCPBodyIsJSONObjectNotBase64 -count=1 stands up an httptest server, drives c.Post(_, map[string]any{...}), and asserts the captured request body parses as a JSON object containing the bound keys.
